name: Git YOLO Linux
description: Bash snippet that commits current work, moves dirty main work to a branch before updating main, and pushes with a force-with-lease retry only for non-main branches.
content:
git_yolo_push() {
  set -e
  main_branch="main"
  remote_name="origin"

  branch="$(git branch --show-current)"
  if [ -z "$branch" ]; then
    echo "Not on a branch."
    return 1
  fi

  git_branch_exists() {
    git show-ref --verify --quiet "refs/heads/$1"
  }

  new_work_branch() {
    prefix="$1"
    stamp="$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)"
    candidate="$prefix-$stamp"
    counter=1
    while git_branch_exists "$candidate"; do
      candidate="$prefix-$stamp-$counter"
      counter=$((counter + 1))
    done
    printf '%s\n' "$candidate"
  }

  update_main() {
    if ! git fetch "$remote_name" "$main_branch"; then
      echo "Could not fetch $remote_name/$main_branch."
      return 1
    fi
    if ! git show-ref --verify --quiet "refs/remotes/$remote_name/$main_branch"; then
      echo "Remote $remote_name/$main_branch was not found."
      return 1
    fi
    git switch "$main_branch" || return 1
    if ! git merge --ff-only "$remote_name/$main_branch"; then
      echo "main cannot be fast-forwarded from $remote_name/$main_branch. Resolve main manually, then retry."
      return 1
    fi
  }

  work_branch="$branch"
  created_from_main=0
  if [ "$branch" = "$main_branch" ] && [ -n "$(git status --porcelain)" ]; then
    work_branch="$(new_work_branch "work/yolo")"
    echo "Local changes are on main; moving them to $work_branch before updating main."
    git switch -c "$work_branch"
    created_from_main=1
  fi

  if [ -n "$(git status --porcelain)" ]; then
    git add -A
    if ! git diff --cached --quiet; then
      git commit -m "chore: yolo update"
    fi
  fi

  if [ "$created_from_main" -eq 1 ]; then
    if ! update_main; then
      git switch "$work_branch" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
      return 1
    fi
    git switch "$work_branch"
    if ! git rebase "$main_branch"; then
      echo "Rebase stopped. Resolve conflicts, then run git rebase --continue or git rebase --abort."
      return 1
    fi
  elif [ "$branch" = "$main_branch" ]; then
    update_main || return 1
    work_branch="$main_branch"
  fi

  if [ "$work_branch" = "$main_branch" ]; then
    if ! git push "$remote_name" "$main_branch"; then
      echo "Main push was rejected. Fetch and inspect before retrying; not force-pushing main."
      return 1
    fi
  elif git rev-parse --abbrev-ref --symbolic-full-name '@{u}' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! git push; then
      echo "Normal push was rejected; retrying with --force-with-lease."
      git push --force-with-lease
    fi
  else
    if ! git push --set-upstream "$remote_name" "$work_branch"; then
      echo "Normal upstream push was rejected; retrying with --force-with-lease."
      git push --set-upstream --force-with-lease "$remote_name" "$work_branch"
    fi
  fi

  git status --short
}

git_yolo_push
